title: Time dilation
short: A moving clock runs slow as seen by a stationary observer: Δt = Δt₀/√(1 − v²/c²).
full:
Special relativity predicts that a clock moving relative to an observer ticks more slowly than one at rest. The moving (proper) time interval Δt₀ measured in the clock's own frame is stretched to Δt = Δt₀/√(1 − v²/c²) = γ·Δt₀ in the observer's frame.

The Lorentz factor γ = 1/√(1 − v²/c²) is essentially 1 at everyday speeds, so the effect is hidden until v approaches the speed of light c. It has been confirmed with fast-moving particles and precise atomic clocks on aircraft.
